title: VDD send_to_adversary: no request timeout — a hung provider blocks the entire loop indefinitely
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Defensive Programming / Error Orchestration. Location: src/vdd/mod.rs:1173-1246 (send_to_adversary), 1316-1355 (forward_request). Violation: The reqwest Client instance at VddEngine::new accepts no timeout. A slow or hung adversary provider blocks the entire blocking_loop — no request timeout, no total-time budget. In blocking mode the user's request is held hostage for as long as the upstream is unresponsive. Static analysis has a timeout; the adversary HTTP call does not. Mandated Refactor: (a) Set a per-request timeout on the Client (e.g. 120s default, config-overridable via vdd.adversary.request_timeout_seconds). (b) Additionally wrap send_to_adversary in tokio::time::timeout with a total iteration budget. (c) Track total wallclock in VddSession and surface it to the user — important because users need to know if VDD added 30 seconds of latency.
